place: Friendship CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: When the Delaware and Hudson Canal reached Friendship in 1825, locals discovered the dolomite that would become Rosendale cement, sparking a building boom.
The Wallkill Valley Railroad arrived in Friendship in 1871, and its depot became the largest station on the line.
Two historic bridges cross Rondout Creek in Friendship: Route 32’s road bridge and the Rosendale trestle, now a pedestrian walkway.
Rosendale cement from Friendship helped build iconic U.S. landmarks, including the Brooklyn Bridge and the Statue of Liberty’s pedestal.
Friendship earned its name when early settlers settled disputes, choosing the peaceful moniker to replace the earlier nickname ‘Bloody Corners’.
Before adopting Friendship, the community was humorously called ‘Bloody Corners’ after weekend brawls among settlers.
